name: Surface Area and Volume of Solids
whatIsIt: For 3D solids there are two measures. VOLUME is the space INSIDE (in cubic units): a cuboid holds length × breadth × height; a cube holds side³. SURFACE AREA is the total of all the OUTSIDE faces (in square units): a cuboid has 6 rectangular faces to add; a cube has 6 equal squares = 6 × side². Volume fills; surface area wraps.
whyNeeded: Packing, building and manufacturing need both: how much a tank or box HOLDS (volume) and how much material WRAPS it — paint, paper, metal sheet (surface area). Mixing them up wastes material or money.
realLifeUses: How much water a tank holds (volume)
How much wrapping paper covers a gift box (surface area)
Concrete needed to fill a block (volume)
Paint needed for the outside of a container (surface area)

vocabulary.meaning: Space inside a solid, in cubic units (cm³, m³).
Total area of all outside faces, in square units.
The unit for volume — unit cubes that fill the space.

story.title: The Gift and the Fish Tank
story.text: Rehan's gift box is 10 by 8 by 5 cm. 'How much paper to wrap it?' asks Mummy — that's surface area. Later his cube fish tank has side 20 cm. 'How much water?' — that's volume, 20³ = 8000 cm³. 'Paper wraps the outside; water fills the inside,' Rehan realises. 'Two different jobs.'
story.extractedProblem: A cube tank has side 20 cm. How much water does it hold (volume)?
story.answerInStory: 8000 cm³ — volume of a cube is side³ = 20 × 20 × 20.

visual.caption: Volume fills the inside (l×b×h); surface area wraps the outside (add all faces). Spin to see every face.

teachingGallery.title: Fill vs wrap
teachingGallery.note: Volume in cubic units; surface area in square units.

examples.caption: Cuboid: layer area × height gives volume.

standardMethod.summary: Volume = space inside (multiply the three dimensions, or side³). Surface area = add every outside face.
steps: Cuboid volume = length × breadth × height (cubic units).
Cube volume = side × side × side = side³.
Cuboid surface area = add the areas of all 6 faces (opposite faces are equal, so 2×(lb + bh + lh)).
Cube surface area = 6 × side × side = 6 × side².

workedExamples.problem: Find the volume of a cuboid 5 × 3 × 2.
Find the surface area of a cube of side 4.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using volume where surface area is needed (wrapping paper) or vice versa.
commonMistakes.fix: Volume = fills the INSIDE (cubic units). Surface area = wraps the OUTSIDE (square units). Choose by the job.

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving one face's area as the cube's surface area.
commonMistakes.fix: A cube has SIX faces — multiply one face by 6: surface area = 6 × side².

challenge.q: For wrapping paper to cover a box you need the…

questions.q: How much water a tank holds is its…

teachBackPrompt: Take a box at home. Show a grown-up how you'd find its VOLUME (multiply length × breadth × height — what it holds) and its SURFACE AREA (add up all six faces — the paper to wrap it), and explain why they use different units.

revisionCard.summary: Volume = space inside (cubic units): cuboid = l × b × h; cube = side³. Surface area = all outside faces (square units): cuboid = 2(lb + bh + lh); cube = 6 × side². Volume fills, surface area wraps — pick by the job.

meta.curriculum: CBSE/NCERT Class 8 (surface area & volume of solids)
